description:
Using PySAM (Python scripting), model a 50 MW DC utility-scale single-axis tracking photovoltaic plant and determine the Year 1 PPA electricity price (in ¢/kWh) that achieves a leveraged after-tax IRR of 11%, using the Single Owner financial model.

System Design Parameters:
- DC Capacity: 50,000 kW
- DC:AC Ratio: 1.2
- Array Type: Single-axis tracking (value 2 in PVWatts)
- Tilt: 0 degrees
- Azimuth: 180 degrees
- System Losses: 14.08%
- Module Type: Standard (value 0)
- Annual Degradation: 0.5%/year
- Weather Data: Use any available TMY weather file from SAM's bundled solar_resource directory (path in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt). Prefer a US Southwest location.

Single Owner Financial Parameters:
- Analysis Period: 25 years
- Federal Tax Rate: 21%
- State Tax Rate: 7%
- Federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC): 30%
- Depreciation: 5-year MACRS
- Inflation Rate: 2.5%
- Real Discount Rate: 6.4%
- Total Installed Cost: $1,100/kW DC ($55,000,000 total)
- Annual Fixed O&M Cost: $16/kW-yr
- Debt Fraction: 54%
- Debt Interest Rate: 5.5% (annual)
- Debt Term: 18 years
- PPA Escalation Rate: 1.0%/year
- PPA Solution Mode: Solve for PPA price to achieve target IRR
- Target IRR (leveraged, after-tax): 11%

Run the performance and financial models together, then save the results as a JSON file to `/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/utility_pv_ppa_results.json`. The JSON must contain these exact keys:
- `weather_file_used` (string)
- `system_capacity_kw` (number)
- `annual_energy_year1_kwh` (number)
- `capacity_factor_percent` (number)
- `ppa_price_year1_cents_per_kwh` (number)
- `leveraged_after_tax_irr_percent` (number)
- `npv_dollars` (number)
- `lcoe_nominal_cents_per_kwh` (number)
- `total_installed_cost_dollars` (number)
